q4.title: Write a query to list student names in descending order.

sk1.title: Sorting Direction in SQL
sk1.bodyMarkdown:
When you use the `ORDER BY` clause in SQL, you can control the direction of sorting with `ASC` (ascending) or `DESC` (descending). By default, `ORDER BY` sorts in ascending order (`ASC`).

- **ASC**: Sorts from smallest to largest (A-Z, 0-9).
- **DESC**: Sorts from largest to smallest (Z-A, 9-0).

**Example:**
```sql
SELECT name, price FROM products
ORDER BY price DESC;
```
This query lists products from the most expensive to the least expensive.

q1.title: Which keyword sorts results from highest to lowest?
q1.prompt: You want to see the most expensive products first. Which keyword should you use with ORDER BY?

q2.title: Select all true statements about ORDER BY direction.
q2.prompt: Which of the following statements about sorting direction in SQL are true?

options.a: `ORDER BY price ASC` sorts prices from lowest to highest.
options.b: `ORDER BY name DESC` sorts names from Z to A.
options.c: `ORDER BY price` is the same as `ORDER BY price ASC`.
options.d: `ORDER BY price DESC` sorts prices from lowest to highest.

q3.title: Arrange the results in ascending order.
q3.prompt: Given the prices: 15, 7, 22, arrange them as they would appear with `ORDER BY price ASC`.

q4.title: Write a query to list student names in descending order.
q4.prompt: Using the `students_intro` dataset, write a SQL query to select all student names in descending alphabetical order.
